title: CI/CD deploy pipeline (GHCR -> homelab, wc-predict.davewil.dev)
description: Replicate the slackex deploy pattern for predictex: GitHub Actions builds a Phoenix release Docker image, pushes to GHCR, SSHes to the homelab Docker host (192.168.1.102 via Tailscale), pulls/migrates/recreates. caddy-docker-proxy serves wc-predict.davewil.dev with TLS via Cloudflare DNS challenge. Postgres on default network only; app joins default + proxy.
notes: Pipeline built, committed, and locally verified (docker build OK, 88 tests, compose valid). All 6 GitHub secrets set (SECRET_KEY_BASE + POSTGRES_PASSWORD generated; DEPLOY_HOST/DEPLOY_SSH_KEY/TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID/TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY copied from Vaultwarden). Remaining: push a v* tag to run the first real deploy end-to-end (Tailscale SSH -> migrate -> caddy TLS for wc-predict.davewil.dev). Close after first green deploy.
close_reason: CI/CD pipeline shipped and proven — deployed v0.2.x, v0.3.0, v0.4.0, v0.4.1 cleanly (build -> GHCR -> Tailscale SSH -> homelab migrate/recreate -> /health). Stale in_progress.

title: LiveView: Admin (results sync, cohort %, players, recompute)
description: Trigger Results.Ingest sync, manual result override, per-fixture FIFA cohort % entry (drives the risky bonus), player management, and recompute scores.
notes: Includes admin manual entry of predictions ON BEHALF OF players (from submitted FIFA screenshots) — the guaranteed-path fallback when auto-import (xox) is unavailable. My Predictions (79q, done) only displays these; entry lives here.
close_reason: Admin console shipped in v0.4.0: prediction entry on behalf of players (by-player + by-fixture audit), fixtures sync/override/cohort, player promote. Reviewed + booster-on-blank fix.

title: LiveView: Leaderboard + WhatsApp export
description: Overall + per-round standings sourced from the DB-backed leaderboard (predictex-0ae), with a 'Copy WhatsApp text' button for sharing in the league chat.
close_reason: Closed

title: Player authentication (phx.gen.auth)
description: Run phx.gen.auth for the Player schema and reconcile with the existing minimal players table (display_name, is_admin already present). Provides login for the ~15 league members.
close_reason: Closed

title: LiveView: My Predictions
description: Read-only personal dashboard at /predictions: a member's imported FIFA picks, per-fixture scoring, booster marker, kickoff lock state, 'no pick imported' warning, rank/total reconciled with the leaderboard, World Cup theme (flags), round tabs, and an outbound FIFA link. NO prediction entry here (admin entry = a02, auto-import = xox).
close_reason: Read-only My Predictions dashboard implemented, reviewed, 216 tests green, runtime + visual smoke confirmed.

title: DB-backed leaderboard
description: Add a Predictex.Standings context that scores DB-backed predictions (players, predictions, fixtures) by reusing the pure Predictex.Scoring engine, and a leaderboard query. Mirror the JSON-based Leaderboard.build/3 logic but source data from Ecto. The existing mix predictex.leaderboard stays as the no-DB tool.
close_reason: Closed

title: Knockout-readiness for live capture (before first KO 2026-06-28)
description:
Group-stage live capture is fully automatic on v0.11.0 (72/104 fixtures have fifa_match_id; the */5 cron arms Workers.LiveScoreSync per in-window fixture). But the 32 KNOCKOUT fixtures are NOT captured: they have no fifa_match_id (producer query skips fifa_match_id IS NULL) and still carry bracket-placeholder teams (2A, 1C, 1E...). First knockout kicks off 2026-06-28 19:00Z (2A v 2B). TWO prerequisites before then:
1. BACKFILL fifa_match_id for the 32 knockout fixtures. Path: Predictex.Fifa.Reference.fetch_rounds() -> Fifa.LiveIds.assign() (the group-stage backfill path), but it can only run once FIFA publishes the knockout match IDs in rounds.json (after the bracket resolves at end of group stage). Verify rounds.json carries KO IdMatch values, then run assign on prod and confirm all 104 fixtures have fifa_match_id. (Related to predictex-i9k, which is the member-import side.)
2. WIDEN the producer window for extra-time/penalties — predictex-cvx (P2 bug): @post_min 150 truncates KO matches going to ET/pens (~155-185min) so the buzz goes dark and is_live sticks true. Bump @post_min to ~210 and/or add an independent is_live auto-clear sweep BEFORE knockouts.
Acceptance: by 2026-06-28, all knockout fixtures have a fifa_match_id AND the producer window covers ET+pens, verified on the first knockout (fifa_captures gains rows through full time + shootout, is_live clears on finish).
acceptance_criteria: All 104 fixtures have fifa_match_id; producer window covers ET+penalties; first knockout (2026-06-28) captured through shootout with is_live clearing on finish
notes:
cvx (knockout-window fix) is code-complete. Before first KO 2026-06-28, VERIFY one openfootball-feed timing assumption underpinning cvx's clear_stuck_live/1 sweep: does the openfootball feed publish the 'ft' (regulation full-time) score — which Results.Openfootball.ft_score/1 maps to status=:completed — only AFTER a knockout fully ends, or can it appear at regulation FT while ET/penalties are still in play? If only-after-end: sweep status-branch is fully safe. If mid-match: the sweep would clear is_live during ET, but because publish is NOT gated on status the live FIFA frames keep re-asserting is_live → degrades to a benign per-tick flicker, not a blackout (this asymmetry is why the publish-side :completed exclusion was deliberately reverted). Check against a real knockout capture (or openfootball's prior-tournament knockout JSON) and record the finding.

DEFERRED FROM predictex-c46 (2026-06-18): the FixtureLive 'Everyone's picks' reveal currently shows scoreline + booster only — group-stage complete. c46's AC user-facing list also called for knockout first-team/first-scorer in the picks reveal. Deferred here because it (a) can't be exercised until the first KO 2026-06-28 and (b) needs the KO data the hco backfill provides (fifa_match_id + resolved teams; first-scorer matching relates to predictex-i9k). Requirement to build: in FixtureLive picks section, when fixture.round.stage is a knockout, render each player's first-team and first-scorer pick alongside scoreline. Source field: Predictions schema knockout columns; reuse the same locked?/anti-copy gate.

title: LiveScoreSync window @post_min 150 truncates knockout ET/penalties -> buzz dark + is_live stuck
description: Whole-branch review of predictex-rfm (auto-capture) found: the producer Workers.LiveScoreSync polls only while kickoff_at is in [now-150min, now+10min]. Knockouts going to extra-time (~155min) or penalties (~170-185min) run PAST the 150-min window. Two consequences: (1) the live buzz stops updating during ET/shootout (the dramatic peak); (2) the last in-window poll wrote is_live:true, the self-reschedule chain then stops, and no finished frame (MatchStatus 0) is ever published, so is_live is left STUCK true. This is a distinct root cause from predictex-d17 (which is endpoint-failure post-match) but the same symptom; the rfm plan's deploy-note claim that it 'closes d17' is OVERSTATED — true only for matches ending <=150min after kickoff (group games do). NOT a group-stage blocker, and knockout fixtures do not yet have fifa_match_id (predictex-i9k), so this is dormant until knockout backfill. Fix options: (a) bump @post_min to ~210 (covers ET+pens+finished frame; cheap one-liner; minor extra polling of finished group games); (b) add an independent windowed is_live auto-clear sweep (robust: also self-heals a dropped/crashed final frame); recommend (b) or (a)+(b). Also correct the d17-closure scope note. Source: lib/predictex/workers/live_score_sync.ex @post_min.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.11.2 (commit 5d8fad4): @post_min 150->210 covers knockout ET/pens + the finished frame; independent clear_stuck_live/1 sweep auto-clears stuck is_live (status=:completed OR past-window). Deployed to prod — Deploy job ran (not skipped), /health 200. End-to-end behavioral validation on a real knockout (ET/shootout + is_live clear) tracked on predictex-hco for first KO 2026-06-28.

title: Auto-start unified live capture (buzz + event-source recording) from schedule
description: We missed RECORDING England v Croatia because capture (FifaLiveCapture/LiveScoreSync) must be armed by hand and stops between matches. Unify + automate: fold the spike's raw-body persistence INTO Workers.LiveScoreSync so one ~30s poll (a) saves the /detail body to fifa_captures (event source), (b) decodes -> live_* on the fixture, (c) broadcasts {:live_update}. Then auto-start from the fixture schedule via an Oban Cron entry that arms the poller ~5-10 min before each fixture.kickoff_at (fixtures with a fifa_match_id), so the buzz goes live automatically AND every match is recorded/replayable. Retire or keep FifaLiveCapture for ad-hoc. RECOMMENDED approach X (unify one worker) per brainstorm 2026-06-17; confirm X vs Y (keep two workers, auto-start both) before building. Also fixes predictex bug where is_live can stay true post-match (a windowed sweep / auto-stop falls out of the scheduler).
design:
Producer/subscriber capture model (refined 2026-06-17, user's framing). ONE worker = the PRODUCER: each ~30s tick fetches ONE /detail snapshot and publishes it on Phoenix.PubSub (e.g. topic 'capture:{match_id}'). Independent SUBSCRIBERS consume it:
 - LiveMatch: decode -> write live_* -> broadcast {:live_update} on 'fixture:{id}' (the buzz; FixtureLive already listens).
 - EventStore: persist the raw body to fifa_captures (the event source that replay reads, predictex-i1s).
 - future: match-day flourishes (predictex-3oo), analytics, a live replay-tee.
Worker knows nothing about its consumers -> add observers without touching it.

TRADE-OFF to settle at build time: Phoenix.PubSub is in-memory + at-most-once. If the EventStore subscriber is down/crashes while handling a message, that snapshot is lost -> a small gap in the recording (replay self-corrects at the next 30s snapshot). For a small friends-group game that's negligible and the symmetric subscriber model is cleanest. If lossless recording ever matters, promote persistence INTO the producer (persist synchronously BEFORE publishing) so the event source is authoritative and only reactive consumers ride PubSub — a localized change. DEFAULT: pure subscriber model; revisit if gaps appear.
notes: PLAN written: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-auto-capture.md (7 TDD tasks, each commit deployable). Producer/subscriber over PubSub as designed; shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder (also for i1s); Capture store promoted from Spike (table fifa_captures kept); Recorder + LiveUpdater subscribers (config-gated off in test); Oban Cron */5 auto-start + unique jobs; pre-kickoff window (kickoff-10min..+150min); retire FifaLiveCapture. Closes the manual-arm gap (missed England) and the is_live-stuck edge predictex-d17.
close_reason: Auto-start unified live capture shipped: 6-task TDD via subagent-driven-development, all on main (4d0c566..7e32c46), full gate green (335 tests). Whole-branch opus review READY-WITH-FIXES, no Critical, 5 invariants hold. Producer/PubSub-subscriber arch: shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder; Spike retired -> permanent Predictex.Capture; Capture.Recorder (event source) + Live.Updater (buzz) subscribers; LiveScoreSync producer publishes on fifa:snapshots, auto-started by Oban Cron */5 with unique:[period:40,states:[:scheduled]] (the only value excluding :executing AND warning-clean on Oban 2.23). Follow-ups filed: predictex-cvx (knockout window truncation, P2), predictex-l3n (LOWs polish, P3). NOT deployed — needs vX.Y.Z tag, user's production-readiness call.

title: Match kickoff times display 1 hour off (BST/timezone not applied)
description: Fixture kickoff times render 1 hour off for UK users — consistent with BST (UTC+1) not being applied. kickoff_at is stored as :utc_datetime (see Fixture schema); the offset suggests times are shown as raw UTC instead of being converted to Europe/London, which is currently UTC+1 (BST, runs late Mar–late Oct, so active during the Jun 11–Jul 19 tournament). Check the display/formatting layer in the LiveViews (e.g. fixture cards, my_predictions, leaderboard) for where kickoff_at is rendered without TZ conversion.
design:
PREFERRED FIX: render kickoff times in the PLAYERS BROWSER timezone, not a server-side zone.

WHY (supersedes the earlier "convert to Europe/London" idea): players may watch from anywhere. A server-side Europe/London conversion fixes UK users but is wrong for everyone else. The browser already knows each viewer's zone + locale, so let the client format. This also makes the original BST symptom a special case of the general fix.

RECOMMENDED APPROACH — pure client-side formatting (no tzdata, no round-trip):
1. Server renders the raw UTC instant in a data attribute / <time> element, e.g. <time data-utc={DateTime.to_iso8601(fixture.kickoff_at)} phx-hook="LocalTime">…</time>.
2. A Phoenix LiveView JS hook (assets/js/app.js) formats on mounted() AND updated() using Intl.DateTimeFormat with NO explicit timeZone (defaults to the browser zone) + the browser locale. DST handled automatically by the browser.
3. Provide a sensible server-rendered fallback inside the <time> (e.g. UTC label) so non-JS / pre-connect render is not blank.

TRADE-OFF (the one real decision): pure client-side means a brief flash of the server fallback (UTC) before the hook runs on the connected LiveView render. Acceptable for a display value; mitigate by rendering "<time> … UTC" as the fallback (honest, not wrong) or visually de-emphasising until formatted. AVOID the heavier alternative (push Intl timezone to the server on connect, format with tzdata) unless the time is needed server-side — it is NOT: all kickoff_at comparisons (round opens_at gating, the LiveScoreSync live-window guard) stay UTC-vs-UTC and correct. Conversion belongs ONLY at the render edge.

SCOPE: find every place kickoff_at is rendered (fixture cards, my_predictions, leaderboard, admin) and route them through the one <time>+hook helper. No migration, no tzdata dependency, no domain logic change.
notes:
SCOPE CONFIRMED 2026-06-17 (grep + read). Render surface is ONE place, not many:

RENDER SITES (all of them):
- lib/predictex_web/components/predictex_components.ex:389-390 — the ONLY formatting helper: defp kickoff/1, uses Calendar.strftime(dt, "%a %d %b · %H:%M"). This is the single point to change.
- Called once, at predictex_components.ex:153, inside the FixtureCard component header ({kickoff(@fx.fixture.kickoff_at)}).
- FixtureCard is consumed in ONE LiveView: lib/predictex_web/live/my_predictions_live.ex:89. Earlier assumption that leaderboard/dashboard/admin also render kickoff is FALSE — they do not. A repo-wide grep found NO other strftime/Calendar/date formatting anywhere in lib/predictex_web. Kickoff time is shown only on My Predictions.

NON-RENDER kickoff_at uses (leave UTC, do NOT touch): predictions.ex:128-129 locked?/2 (UTC compare), dashboard.ex:21 order_by, results/openfootball.ex + ingest.ex (ingest), fifa/crosswalk.ex (match key). All correct as UTC.

IMPLEMENTATION DETAIL — uses Phoenix 1.8 COLOCATED HOOKS: assets/js/app.js does `import {hooks as colocatedHooks} from "phoenix-colocated/predictex"` and `hooks: {...colocatedHooks}`. So the new LocalTime hook should be COLOCATED in predictex_components.ex via a <script :type={Phoenix.LiveView.ColocatedHook} name=".LocalTime"> block next to the markup — NO app.js edit required (supersedes the design-note suggestion to add a hook to app.js).

EDIT SHAPE:
1. predictex_components.ex:153 — replace {kickoff(...)} with a <time datetime={DateTime.to_iso8601(dt)} phx-hook=".LocalTime"> carrying a server fallback (e.g. the current strftime output, or "… UTC"). Keep kickoff(nil) -> "TBC".
2. Colocated LocalTime hook: format el via Intl.DateTimeFormat(undefined, {weekday:"short", day:"2-digit", month:"short", hour:"2-digit", minute:"2-digit"}) on mounted() + updated() — browser zone, matches the "%a %d %b · %H:%M" style.
No migration, no tzdata, no app.js change. One component + one colocated hook.
close_reason: Fixed via idiomatic server-side per-viewer timezone (not the initial client JS hook, which was reworked out). JS reports the browser IANA zone once via LiveSocket _tz connect param + a tz cookie; a :browser plug copies the cookie to session; PredictexWeb.TimeZone on_mount assigns @tz (connect-param when connected, session/cookie on dead render, Etc/UTC fallback) to the :require_authenticated_player live_session; pure testable PredictexWeb.TimeHelpers.kickoff/2 shifts UTC->local via the tz lib (defensive UTC fallback on bad/spoofed zones). local_time/1 renders server-localized <time>. Commit 1e0e125 (supersedes client-hook 717a663). Opus review approved; 347 tests, assets.build clean. On main locally; NOT deployed (needs tag).

title: Import (/import) has no discoverable route — players can't find how to get started
description: The /import flow (predictex-4ar, shipped v0.8.0) is live but UNLINKED: nav has Leaderboard / My Picks / Log in / Join the league, with no entry to /import. New players land on the leaderboard with no obvious 'import your picks / get started' path. Add a discoverable entry: a nav link for authenticated players and/or a prominent CTA on the My Picks page (esp. empty state). Onboarding gap.
close_reason: Added a primary 'Import picks' nav link (→/import) for authenticated players in root.html.heex — discoverable from every page. Verified in browser.

title: Leaderboard mislabels points as 'fixtures' on champion card
description: The champion spotlight on /(LeaderboardLive) renders '{fixtures_total} fixtures · {round_bonus_total} bonus' (leaderboard_live.ex:70). But fixtures_total is POINTS earned from fixture predictions (Standings: sum of result.fixture_total), not a count of fixtures — so the leader shows e.g. '250 fixtures' when 250 is a points total and there are only ~104 fixtures total. Misleading. Fix: relabel to make clear it's points from fixtures (e.g. '250 from fixtures · 0 bonus'). Check the leaderboard_row component for the same fixtures/bonus columns.
notes: Second instance of the same mislabel found & fixed: my_predictions_live.ex:56 (My Picks dashboard) also read '{n} fixtures · {n} bonus' → now 'from fixtures'. Regression assertion added to my_predictions_live_test.exs too. Both card labels now consistent.
close_reason: Champion card now reads 'N from fixtures · N bonus' (was 'N fixtures', misread as a count); row columns get title tooltips clarifying fixture vs bonus points. Regression test added; verified in browser. Fixed in leaderboard_live.ex:70 + predictex_components.ex.

title: Mum-proof FIFA import guide (esp. mobile flow)
description:
A dead-simple, friendly, step-by-step guide for importing your FIFA picks — clear enough that a non-technical 75-year-old could follow it unaided. The 'mum test' is the bar.

Current /import UX is the problem: it says 'Drag this button to your bookmarks bar' (desktop-only) and the fallback is 'run it in the browser console and paste the JSON it prints here' — pure developer jargon, and the console path is basically impossible on a phone. Most of the group will be on mobile in the WhatsApp chat, so the mobile path needs the most thought.

Goal: platform-aware, illustrated, plain-language onboarding with a 'did it work?' confirmation — no scary words ('bookmarklet', 'JSON', 'console' all banned from the user-facing copy).
design:
- Detect platform (desktop vs mobile) and show the RIGHT flow only — don't make the user choose.
- DESKTOP: numbered steps with screenshots/short gifs — (1) drag the 'Import my picks' button to your bookmarks bar, (2) open FIFA and sign in, (3) click the button, (4) we show you what we found. Rename the bookmarklet to plain language ('Import my picks'); never say 'bookmarklet'.
- MOBILE (the hard part) — candidate flows, pick/validate one:
  1. Screenshot → admin entry: user screenshots their FIFA picks page and sends it to the group admin, who already enters predictions from screenshots (existing admin flow). Zero-tech for the user; arguably the true mum-path on mobile.
  2. Copy-paste: copy the FIFA data and paste into /import — but obtaining the data on mobile (no console) is the blocker; needs a share/copy affordance from FIFA that we can't control.
  3. Guided mobile add-bookmark: iOS Safari 'add bookmark then edit the address to start with the magic text' — fiddly, fails the mum test; likely reject.
  4. Server-side fetch from a per-user FIFA share URL: user pastes a link, server fetches — gated by same-origin/auth (already hit this 'same-origin fetch gate').
- Replace the console-fallback copy entirely with mum-friendly wording regardless of flow.
- Relates to existing import work: ImportLive (/import), the colocated .FifaFragment hook, and [[predictex-i9k]] (knockout import).
acceptance_criteria:
- On desktop, a first-time user can import following only the on-screen steps, no outside help.
- On mobile, there is a clearly-signposted path that completes an import (or routes to the admin-screenshot path) without needing a console or bookmarklet drag.
- User-facing copy contains no developer jargon ('bookmarklet', 'JSON', 'console').
- A clear success/confirmation state tells the user their picks are in.
notes: CORRECTION: earlier 'DEPLOYED & VERIFIED' note was WRONG — those were plain pushes to main, which SKIP the tag-gated Deploy job (see memory deploy-requires-a-git-tag). The 4ar feature only actually reached prod with tag v0.8.0 (2026-06-17), Deploy job completed/success, verified live. The intermediate prod checks passed only because the /import route pre-existed from xox.
close_reason: Group-stage mum-proof FIFA import shipped & deployed to prod (run 27661365740, health 200). Smoke-tested in real browser; one bug (placeholder interpolation) found & fixed. Follow-ups tracked: dnp/066/fs6/xww.

title: Source live in-play fixture scores (feed spike)
description:
Spike: find and validate a source of LIVE in-play scores for World Cup 2026 fixtures. The existing Predictex.Results.Ingest / ResultSync pulls FINAL results from the openfootball feed; the live drill-down/buzz feature (predictex-c46) needs the current score WHILE a match is in progress.

Investigate: does openfootball expose in-play scores, or do we need another feed (FIFA match-predictor API already used for cohort/import? a live-scores API?)? Determine update cadence, rate limits, fixture-id crosswalk, and whether a polling Oban cron (like ResultSync) suffices. Fallback: admin-entered live score as a manual stopgap.

Output: a recommendation + the Fixture.status :live transition wiring (status enum already supports :live).
design:
LIVE IN-PLAY SCORE FEED — RECOMMENDATION (scouted 2026-06-17, tournament already live)

FINDING: openfootball CANNOT serve live scores. worldcup.json is wiki/manually updated; its own README states data is NOT live-updated. Our Results.Ingest (lib/predictex/results/ingest.ex) pulls FINAL results only every 15 min (ResultSync cron */15). So a separate live source is required.

== PHASE A: BUILD NOW (source-agnostic, the durable part) ==
New LiveScoreSync Oban worker mirroring CohortSync (lib/predictex/workers/cohort_sync.ex): injectable source_fun, Gather->Decide->Act.
- Cron: every minute, but GUARDED — short-circuit with NO API call unless >=1 fixture is in a live window (kickoff_at <= now <= kickoff_at+150min). Bounds requests to match-day windows.
- Act: update ONLY status::live + provisional home_goals/away_goals via Tournament.update_fixture/2.
- AUTHORITY RULE (critical): openfootball/ResultSync stays the SOLE authority for FINAL results + first_scorer (these feed scoring/standings). The live feed must NOT write canonical completed/scored data — only the in-play :live provisional score for the buzz UI. On feed full-time, let the next openfootball sync confirm canonical score + first-scorer. Prevents a flaky live feed from corrupting scored standings.
- Crosswalk: reuse existing Fifa.Crosswalk.match_key/3 ({utc_date, team MapSet}) — zero schema change, same approach as CohortSync. No new external-id column needed.
- Fallback: expose home_goals/away_goals/status edit in admin_fixtures_live.ex for manual live entry. Schema already supports it; low effort; degradation path if a feed dies mid-tournament.

== PHASE B: PICK THE FEED (deferred decision; validate against a real live match) ==
Opt 1 FIFA-native undocumented (PROBE FIRST, zero cost): app already integrates play.fifa.com CDN + FIFA crosswalk. FIFA site is driven by undocumented api.fifa.com/api/v3 (historically a liveNow filter, matchId-keyed). If it works for 2026 it is the winner: no new dependency, reuse crosswalk, free. RISK: undocumented/unstable. Probe-able NOW (matches live since Jun 11).
Opt 2 football-data.org EUR12/mo livescores (RECOMMENDED paid primary): reputable, stable, 10 req/min (ample w/ windowed polling — one call returns all live matches), clean fixtures+team names -> existing crosswalk. ~EUR12-24 total, cancel after Jul 19. Best reliability-per-euro.
Opt 3 BALLDONTLIE FIFA (fifa.balldontlie.io): nicest in-play fields (clock_display 47:15, status, match_number maps cleanly to openfootball). Live needs paid ($9.99 ALL-STAR verify matches incl., $39.99 GOAT all). Free tier 5 req/min is teams/stadiums only.
Opt 4 worldcup26.ir (rezarahiminia free OSS): free, no key, 500 req/min, claims real-time but UNPROVEN, single-maintainer, no SLA. OK as best-effort ONLY paired with admin manual fallback, not sole source.
AVOID: API-Football free (100 req/day too low for live polling); Sportmonks (no permanent free tier).

RECOMMENDATION: Build Phase A against the abstraction now. Probe FIFA-native endpoint against a real live match (possible today); if it does not pan out, use football-data.org EUR12/mo as reliable primary. Admin manual entry is the always-available fallback. Provider choice is a swap-one-module change thanks to source_fun injection.
notes:
FIFA-NATIVE PROBE RESULTS (2026-06-17 13:11Z) — THIS IS THE RECOMMENDED SOURCE, supersedes the paid-feed options.

api.fifa.com/api/v3 is LIVE, FREE, NO API KEY. Two endpoints we need:

A) LIVE POLL (lightweight): GET https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/live/football/now
   -> {Results:[...]} = only matches currently in play across ALL FIFA comps; empty [] when none (verified empty at 13:11Z, no WC match in play). Filter Results by IdCompetition=="17". This is FIFAs own match-centre live feed -> designed to tick in real time. IDEAL for the windowed LiveScoreSync poll.

B) FULL WC CALENDAR: GET https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/calendar/matches?idCompetition=17&idSeason=285023&count=200&language=en
   -> all 104 WC2026 matches. Per match: IdMatch, HomeTeamScore/AwayTeamScore, MatchStatus, MatchTime ("97\x27" minute string), Period, Winner, HomeTeamPenaltyScore/AwayTeamPenaltyScore, Date (UTC Z), Home/Away.TeamName.

MatchStatus enum decoded from live data: 0 = FINISHED (has scores + MatchTime), 1 = UPCOMING (scores null). LIVE is a 3rd code (not yet observed; classic FIFA value is 3) + Period/MatchTime populated. <-- ONLY remaining unknown.

CROSSWALK IS TRIVIAL: v3 IdMatch (e.g. 400021496) == the fifaId field ALREADY in the rounds.json our app pulls (Fifa.Reference: play.fifa.com/json/match_predictor/rounds.json carries fifaId + homeScore/awayScore + matchcentreUrl /match/17/285023/289273/400021443). So: 17=idCompetition, 285023=idSeason, 289273=idStage. Either (a) match v3 IdMatch to rounds.json fifaId, or (b) add a one-time fifa_id column to Fixture sourced from rounds.json, or (c) reuse existing Fifa.Crosswalk.match_key (Date+TeamName). Zero fragile guessing.

IMPACT ON RECOMMENDATION: FIFA-native wins — free, no key, reuses Req + FIFA crosswalk already in the app, gives live minute (MatchTime) for the buzz UI. football-data.org EUR12/mo demoted to fallback-B (only if FIFA proves flaky mid-tournament). Admin manual entry stays as always-available stopgap. Authority rule UNCHANGED: openfootball/ResultSync remains sole authority for FINAL score + first_scorer (scoring); FIFA live feed only sets status :live + provisional in-play score.

REMAINING TO CONFIRM (needs an in-play match; next = Portugal v Congo DR 17:00Z today): exact live MatchStatus code + that live/football/now scores update in real time. Low risk (it powers fifa.com live). After that, the spike is fully closed and ready to build LiveScoreSync against endpoint A.
close_reason: Spike complete: FIFA v3 live contract decoded (live MatchStatus=3 confirmed from the Portugal v DR Congo capture, 696 rows), capture worker shipped. Its persistence folds into predictex-rfm (FifaLiveCapture retired there). c46 shipped on top.

title: Live fixture drill-down + what-if leaderboard buzz
description:
When a fixture is LIVE, let a member expand/drill into it to see the social 'what-if' picture in real time — the core group-chat buzz loop from the design brief ('Predict the scores. Win the group chat.').

User-facing behaviour:
- Expand/drill into a live fixture (in-place expand on a 'Live now' section of the leaderboard, or a /fixtures/:id view) to see EVERYONE's locked predictions for that fixture (scoreline, booster, and in knockouts first-team/first-scorer).
- Show how the CURRENT live score would change the leaderboard — a provisional/projected standings computed as if the fixture finished at the current score (not persisted).
- Generate 'buzz' scenarios: e.g. 'if Brazil scores, Sav moves to #1', 'if it ends 2–1, you overtake Dave'. Surface the rank deltas for a few plausible next outcomes (home scores → h+1, away scores → a+1, full-time-at-current).

Why: this is the headline social feature — it turns a live match into shared drama in the group chat. High engagement value during match days.
design:
- Prediction visibility: only reveal others' picks AFTER kickoff (gate on Dashboard/Predictions.locked?). Showing picks before kickoff would let players copy — hard rule.
- Projected scoring: add a pure 'projection' mode to Predictex.Standings/Scoring — e.g. Standings.project(fixture_id, home, away) returning a projected leaderboard WITHOUT persisting. Must honour booster (2x) and risky/cohort bonus for the projection to be accurate (or document the simplification).
- Scenario set: compute leaderboard for {end-at-current, home+1, away+1} and diff vs current standings to extract rank-change narratives.
- Real-time: ideally re-project via PubSub as the live score ticks — shares infra with [[predictex-3oo]] (event-triggered flourishes / PubSub on results sync).
- Reuses standings compute heavily → relates to [[predictex-a4j]] (cache/scope Standings.leaderboard/0). Needs current_scope to phrase 'you overtake X' → relates to [[predictex-kzz]] ('YOU' highlight on public leaderboard).
- Placement TBD: 'Live now' card on the leaderboard front door vs dedicated drill-down route.
acceptance_criteria:
- A live fixture is drillable; non-live fixtures are not.
- Drill-down lists all players' picks for that fixture, only once the fixture has kicked off.
- A projected leaderboard reflects the current live score (booster + risky bonus honoured), computed without persisting.
- At least the 'if it ends now' and 'if home/away scores next' scenarios produce correct rank-change statements.
- 'YOU' framing works for the logged-in viewer.
notes: REDESIGN SHIPPED v0.10.2: FixtureLive rebuilt on the design system — projected what-if standings with ▲/▼ movement arrows, ⚡ buzz headlines ('X moves up to #N'), team flags, font-score, LIVE pulse, 'you' highlight. Buzz engine extended (scenarios_with_deltas/3, headlines/4). Demo data: Predictex.Demo (tagged @demo.predictex.local, purge/0 removes) — FIXED scoreline variety (was all 1-0 -> no movement; now palette generator so 6 players diverge per fixture -> real overtakes). Verified live on prod fixture 61 (Portugal v DR Congo): headlines fire, ranks shuffle. 333 tests. NOTE: built on the design SYSTEM, not the Claude Design mockup (link is auth-gated/403 — file never obtained). OPEN for England v Croatia 20:00Z: poller must be re-armed after kickoff (rpc LiveScoreSync.start()) since it stops between match windows — demo players already have varied predictions on that fixture.
close_reason: Live fixture drill-down + what-if buzz shipped v0.10.2, live on prod. All AC met for group stage: live-gated drillability (UI entry points gate on is_live), post-kickoff anti-copy picks reveal, projected leaderboard honouring booster+risky (Standings.project/3 reuses real rank/2 path), end-now/home-next/away-next scenarios with tested rank-change statements (buzz_test.exs AC#4), and YOU framing. 11 tests pass. Knockout first-team/first-scorer picks display deferred to predictex-hco (can't exercise until first KO 2026-06-28; needs hco backfill data).

title: Implement Predictex.dc.html design (theme + screens)
description: Apply the claude.ai/design handoff bundle (Predictex.dc.html) to the live app: daisyUI pitch-green+gold theme, Archivo/Spline fonts, FixtureCard+LeaderboardRow atoms, Leaderboard champion-spotlight, My Predictions, Auth, Admin visual language, flourish keyframes. Dark default. Preserve all phx- bindings/ids/hooks.
close_reason: Implemented Predictex.dc.html design: themed app.css (pitch-green/gold, dark default) + fonts, fixture_card/leaderboard_row/auth_card atoms, champion-spotlight leaderboard, branded My Predictions/auth/navbar/admin. 275 tests pass; verified in browser. Deferred items filed as follow-ups.

title: Auto-sync FIFA cohort % (risky-bonus data) from matchStats.json
description: Spike (docs/superpowers/research/2026-06-16-xox-fifa-import-spike.md) found FIFA publishes per-outcome cohort percentages at play.fifa.com/json/match_predictor/matchStats.json, keyed by matchId, server-fetchable (HTTP 200 plain curl, NOT Akamai-gated). homeWin/draw/awayWin map exactly to cohort_home_pct/cohort_draw_pct/cohort_away_pct. Today these are admin-entered (a02) and the risky bonus is silently skipped when unset. Build a plain Oban worker (mt6 substrate): Req.get matchStats.json + rounds.json, map matchId -> Fixture (crosswalk by kickoff date/teams), upsert cohort_*_pct. No bookmarklet, no member action. Also exposes firstGoalIsOwn + results as a cross-check. Separate from xox (public stats, not member predictions).
close_reason: Shipped in v0.5.0. mt6: hourly... ResultSync Oban worker (every 15m) runs Ingest.sync_from_url. 7ux: CohortSync Oban worker (hourly) pulls FIFA matchStats.json, pure Fifa.Cohort.plan joins by {utc_date,team-set} + orientation, overwrites cohort_*_pct.

title: Automated result-sync schedule
description: Run Results.Ingest on a schedule (Oban or a Task) so fixtures/results refresh automatically during the tournament. Verify the source ft excludes ET before trusting it for knockout scoring.
close_reason: Shipped in v0.5.0. mt6: hourly... ResultSync Oban worker (every 15m) runs Ingest.sync_from_url. 7ux: CohortSync Oban worker (hourly) pulls FIFA matchStats.json, pure Fifa.Cohort.plan joins by {utc_date,team-set} + orientation, overwrites cohort_*_pct.

title: FIFA prediction import (bookmarklet + /api/import)
description: Auth-safe import: bookmarklet runs in the player's own play.fifa.com session, POSTs only prediction JSON to /api/import authenticated with our session. Capture the FIFA endpoint via DevTools (site 403s scripted requests). Paste-JSON and manual entry fallbacks.
notes: Group-stage FIFA import SHIPPED in v0.8.0 (platform-aware /import built on this in predictex-4ar): desktop bookmarklet + mobile per-round copy-paste, Fifa.Import.plan/rows_from_envelope, paste + fragment-handoff fallbacks. Live on prod, smoke-validated in browser (real round-1 FIFA envelope -> live crosswalk -> preview -> per-round DB write). Knockout import split to predictex-i9k. Honest gap: the desktop bookmarklet's in-FIFA-session fetch click-through wasn't run against a live FIFA login during the smoke (paste path + fragment-handoff were validated; spike confirmed the endpoint works in-session). Note: predictex-2ww proposes replacing member self-import with native in-app entry, which would supersede this surface.
close_reason: Group-stage import shipped & live (v0.8.0); knockout tracked in predictex-i9k. Member self-import may later be superseded by native entry (predictex-2ww).

title: Live game on /predictions is a CTA to its /fixtures/:id live page
description: On the predictions page (MyPredictionsLive), when a fixture is live (live_buzz flag on AND fixture.is_live), make the LIVE badge on its FixtureCard a tappable CTA linking (navigate) to the live drill-down at /fixtures/:id (FixtureLive), so members can jump straight from their predictions to the live what-if view. Contained link on the badge (the card is a div but contains the fifa_url anchor, so do NOT wrap the whole card — avoid nested <a>). Only shown when live; non-live cards unchanged. Note: user referred to it as /live/:matchid but the actual route is /fixtures/:id keyed on local fixture id.
close_reason: Live game on /predictions is now a CTA: when :live_buzz on AND fixture.is_live, the LIVE badge on the FixtureCard is a contained <.link navigate> to /fixtures/:id (the live drill-down); non-live cards unchanged; no nested anchor. live_path passed as a component attr from my_predictions (which has ~p). Commit d957a3f; tested (href present when live, absent when not). On main (pushed); NOT deployed.

title: rfm follow-up: capture-system polish (test coverage + GenServer hygiene)
description: Deferred LOW findings from the predictex-rfm whole-branch review (none blocking): (1) Predictex.Capture.summary/analyze/format are now public rpc-only helpers with no test coverage in the new home — port the 3 analysis-helper tests deleted from spike_test.exs (alias swap Spike->Capture, record_capture->record_snapshot). (2) LiveScore.apply_to_fixture/2 always calls Tournament.update_fixture even when nothing changed — add a changed? guard to skip the redundant ~30s UPDATE per in-window fixture. (3) Capture.Recorder discards _fixture_id silently — add a one-word comment (rows key on FIFA match_id, not local id). (4) Neither Capture.Recorder nor Live.Updater has a handle_info catchall — add cheap insurance against a future publisher on the fifa:snapshots topic. (5) recorder_test uses Process.sleep(50); a GenServer.call sync barrier would be deterministic.

title: Admin 'Start/Stop demo replay' button (fast-follow to replay engine)
description: Once the rpc-driven replay engine exists, add an admin-only control to start/stop a replay without the console, mounted behind :require_admin (mirror the FunWithFlags dashboard pattern at /admin/feature-flags). Makes audience demos one-tap. Out of scope of the replay-engine spec; depends on it.

title: Match replay engine — replay any recorded match (stream-based) onto a demo fixture
description: SPEC: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-match-replay-demo-design.md. Replay the event-sourced fifa_captures of ANY recorded match as a lazy Stream (Stream.cycle for loop + Stream.zip(Stream.interval(interval_ms)) for pacing; default 1s ~= 30x speed), consumed by a stoppable host task, applied via a shared Predictex.LiveScore decoder (extracted from LiveScoreSync) to an ISOLATED demo fixture (the ONLY legal target; never a real fixture). Drives the existing /fixtures/:id buzz UI via PubSub so all viewers see it. rpc-first: Replay.start/stop. Generalized from demo-only to any match_id (no hardcoded default once unified capture lands). Needs: shared LiveScore decoder + Predictex.Demo extended with a demo round+fixture (past kickoff so picks reveal). Replay of the existing 400021502 capture works today; recording NEW matches depends on the unified-capture issue.
notes: CAPTURE GAP (verified on prod 2026-06-18 via Capture.Snapshot counts): England v Croatia (fifa_match_id 400021507, kickoff 2026-06-17 20:00Z) has ZERO capture rows, so it is NOT replayable — this is EXPECTED, not a bug. The match finished (~21:50Z) ~2h before the auto-capture pipeline (predictex-rfm) shipped: v0.11.0 was tagged 2026-06-17 23:54Z and deployed minutes later. Pre-v0.11.0, capture required manually arming the poller (rpc LiveScoreSync.start()), which wasn't done for EvC. Matches recorded and replayable: Ghana v Panama (400021510, 170 rows, pipeline came up mid-match) and Uzbekistan v Colombia (400021504, 318 rows, first full end-to-end auto capture) — plus the original spike match 400021502. Implication for replay: when the engine generalizes to 'replay any match', guard against zero-row match_ids (return a clear 'no capture timeline for this match' rather than an empty/erroring stream). Scoring is unaffected — openfootball is the scoring authority; the capture store is only the live status/score/buzz timeline.

title: LiveScoreSync: is_live can stay true if FIFA detail endpoint stops returning 200 post-match
description: LiveScoreSync.apply_update only runs on {:ok, 200, body}; any other response hits the log-and-skip branch, leaving is_live frozen true until the fixture exits the 150-min window (after which it's never revisited). Per the fifa-v3-live-api-contract a finished match returns MatchStatus 0 with scores at a 200, which clears is_live correctly — so this only bites if FIFA 404s/500s a just-finished match before a clean 0 is observed. The original spec's /now-membership design would have treated 'match disappeared' as the finish signal. Options: (a) a stale-window sweep that clears is_live for fixtures past kickoff+window, (b) fall back to /now membership for finish detection. Watch during first live enablement (Portugal v Congo DR was the first scheduled live test). Surfaced by the predictex-c46 whole-branch review.
notes: Root cause (endpoint stops returning 200 post-match → is_live stuck true) is now self-healed by predictex-cvx's clear_stuck_live/1 sweep in Workers.LiveScoreSync: the status-branch clears is_live within ~one ResultSync cycle (≤15min) once openfootball marks status=:completed, even while the fixture is still in the (DB-query) capture window and the chain is alive — exactly the d17 scenario. Covered by the 'd17 fast-path' unit test (completed fixture, chain running → is_live cleared). Close together with cvx after the same prod validation; keeping open as the explicit d17 acceptance check.
close_reason: Self-healed by predictex-cvx's clear_stuck_live/1 status-branch: once openfootball marks status=:completed, the sweep clears is_live within ~one ResultSync cycle even while the producer chain is alive and regardless of FIFA endpoint health — exactly the d17 endpoint-stall root cause. Shipped + deployed in v0.11.2; covered by the d17 fast-path unit test. Prod validation shared with hco.

title: Pivot: run the game natively in-app (members predict here); keep only FIFA risky/cohort import
description:
STRATEGIC PIVOT (deferred — 'for later', not yet committed). Today members make their picks ON FIFA and pull them into Predictex via the self-import flow (predictex-xox/4ar: bookmarklet + mobile copy-paste). Proposal: members enter predictions DIRECTLY in this app (native scoreline + booster per fixture), removing the FIFA round-trip for picks entirely.

KEEP importing FIFA's 'risky' data: the public cohort / popular-pick stats (matchStats.json homeWin/draw/awayWin %, quickPicks) that drive the risky bonus. That's already server-fetchable and handled by the CohortSync Oban worker — no member action, no auth gate — so it survives the pivot untouched.

NET: the only remaining FIFA dependency becomes the public risky/cohort feed; member predictions become first-class in Predictex.
design:
Native prediction entry for members:
- Per-fixture scoreline entry (home/away goals) + one booster per round, mirroring the existing Prediction contract and admin entry (predictex-a02) but self-service for the logged-in player.
- Reuse existing scoring (Scoring/Standings) and kickoff lockout — picks lock at kickoff; pre-kickoff editable. Validate at the boundary (anti-corruption layer) per LiveView discipline.
- Likely a new member-facing edit LiveView (or extend My Picks /predictions from read-only to editable), replacing the 'Edit on FIFA' links.

Keep: CohortSync worker + matchStats.json risky/cohort import (drives risky bonus).

Deprecate if this lands (supersedes): the member self-import surface — /import flow, Fifa.Import.plan + rows_from_envelope, bookmarklet, ImportLive — and its open follow-ups predictex-066 (iOS Shortcut), predictex-dnp (import screenshots), predictex-xww (screenshot OCR), predictex-fs6 (resume import). Do NOT close those now; mark superseded only when this is committed.

Open questions for when picked up: do members still mirror picks to FIFA (dual-entry), or is Predictex the sole source? Migration of any already-imported picks? Knockout first-scorer entry UI.

title: Add illustrative screenshots to mobile import steps
description: The mobile /import flow (predictex-4ar) has [screenshot: ...] HEeX comment slots in the Select-all/Copy step. Capture real screenshots from a live FIFA session (Android long-press -> Select all -> Copy) and a predictex paste-back, add as static assets under priv/static/images/, reference from import_live.ex. Operator capture work; deferred from 4ar at user request.

title: Resume mobile import at the first unsaved round after a tab discard
description: ImportLive.mount hardcodes current_round=1 and never reads existing predictions. After a mobile tab discard the DATA survives (durable per-round writes), but the UI restarts at round 1 so the user re-pastes already-saved rounds (idempotent, but annoying). Make mount read the player's saved group-stage predictions and resume at the first round with no saved picks. Follow-up from predictex-4ar code review.

title: iOS Shortcut import path (Run JavaScript on Web Page)
description: Smoother iPhone import via a Share-Sheet Shortcut that runs the same in-session fetch as the bookmarklet. Needs an iPhone-owning group member to validate the full cold-start (enable-scripts toggle + per-domain permission prompt) before committing. Enhancement over the copy-paste floor shipped in predictex-4ar.

title: Remove default Phoenix scaffold cruft (logo, favicon, title suffix)
description: Strip Phoenix scaffold branding so the project reads as pure Predictex: removed priv/static/images/logo.svg (Phoenix wordmark, unused after navbar brand_logo swap) and the default Phoenix favicon.ico; added a Predictex SVG favicon (brand mark); changed the live_title suffix from ' · Phoenix Framework' to ' · Predictex'; pruned static_paths. (Navbar 'Website/GitHub/Get Started' links were already removed in the v0.7.0 redesign.)
close_reason: Done: Phoenix logo/favicon removed, Predictex favicon added, title suffix + static_paths fixed. 275 tests pass; no stale references.

title: Highlight current player ('YOU') on the public leaderboard
description: leaderboard_row/1 already supports a 'you' attr (green highlight + YOU badge). Not wired on the public '/' route because LeaderboardLive has no current_scope (route is in a bare scope, not a live_session with mount_current_scope). Add mount_current_scope on_mount to the '/' route and pass you={s.player_id == current_scope.player.id} when logged in.

title: Event-triggered match-day flourishes (rank climb / exact / booster toast on results sync)
description: Design section 07 + footer item ②: celebratory CSS toasts on match-day events. The keyframes (.animate-pdx-rise/-pop/-glow) and .font-score ship in app.css and a static rise is applied to the champion hero. The triggering is backend work: detect rank-climb / exact / booster-hit on results sync and broadcast (PubSub) a toast to the player. Wire as a daisyUI toast + animate- utility.

title: Per-fixture points breakdown + risky-pick banner on FixtureCard
description: Design (Predictex.dc.html, footer item ③) shows per-fixture scoring breakdown chips (Outcome +10 / Home +5 / Away +5 / GD +5 / Exact +5) and a 'Risky pick paid off — only N% backed it' banner. Deferred from the design implementation because Dashboard exposes only total fixture points (not the component breakdown) and no risky flag. Needs Predictex.Scoring to expose a per-prediction breakdown map + Dashboard to surface it, then render the chips in fixture_card/1 (extension point already in place).

title: xox knockout import + first-scorer matching
description: Extend FIFA import to knockout rounds (4-8): resolve firstSquadScored->side and firstPlayerScored->player name (fuzzy cross-source match vs openfootball). Blocked until knockout rounds open and a populated prediction/show sample exists. Group-stage import shipped under xox; see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-16-xox-fifa-import-design.md (Out of scope).

title: Admin by-fixture inline prediction editing
description: The admin by-fixture lens (/admin/predictions?view=fixture) is currently read-only (audit: shows each player's pick + flags missing). The spec (2026-06-15-admin-console-design.md) originally called for inline save here too. Defer-decision during a02: by-player entry already covers all data entry, so by-fixture save was scoped out. To build: turn each player row into an editable form (home/away goals, first_scorer_side + first_scorer_player, booster) saving via Predictions.admin_upsert_prediction/1 (already exists, currently only called from tests; it handles the transactional booster-clear). Watch the one-booster-per-round rule across the by-fixture view.

title: Cache/scope Standings.leaderboard/0 (recomputed per dashboard load)
description: Predictex.Dashboard.for_player/2 calls Standings.leaderboard/0 on every /predictions page load, which scores all players × all predictions × all completed fixtures just to extract one player's entry and rank — O(league size) per view, no caching. Fine for the current ~15-player league; revisit (ETS cache, per-request memo, or a scoped query) if the league grows or page load gets slow.

title: Flags: commit openfootball team-name snapshot + regression test
description: PredictexWeb.Flags is keyed on the live openfootball 2026 feed strings, verified once by fetch-and-diff during implementation. CI cannot hit the live feed, so coverage can silently rot as WC2026 placeholders (e.g. W73, 1A) resolve to real nations that then fall back to ⚽. Commit a snapshot of the feed's team-name list as a test fixture and assert the flag map covers every real nation in it. Cosmetic-only impact (unmapped → ⚽), hence low priority.
notes: Partially addressed by 7ux: lib/predictex/fifa/cohort.ex @aliases is a data-verified FIFA<->openfootball name map (8 divergences diffed from live squads.json vs worldcup.json). Remaining c9s work: commit an openfootball team-name snapshot + regression test.

title: Deferred decision: persist event source IN the capture producer (lossless recording)
description:
DEFERRED ALTERNATIVE to the default pure-subscriber capture model in predictex-rfm. We chose the pure producer/subscriber model (worker publishes; LiveMatch + EventStore are independent PubSub subscribers) for symmetry/simplicity. RISK accepted: Phoenix.PubSub is in-memory + at-most-once, so if the EventStore subscriber is down/crashes on a message, that snapshot is dropped -> a small gap in the authoritative recording that replay (predictex-i1s) reads. At 30s cadence + friends-group scale this is negligible and self-corrects next tick.

TRIGGER to revisit: replay shows gaps/jumps, OR recording integrity becomes important (e.g. monetised, multi-tenant, or analytics that need every snapshot).

WHAT THE DEFERRED OPTION REQUIRES: move persistence INTO the producer — the worker writes the raw body to fifa_captures SYNCHRONOUSLY before broadcasting, so the event source is guaranteed lossless; the standalone EventStore subscriber is then removed/redundant and only reactive consumers (LiveMatch, flourishes) ride PubSub. Localized change to the capture worker; no schema change. Decided 2026-06-17.

title: FixtureLive polish: scenario header casing vs buzz chip casing
description: On /fixtures/:id the scenario headers render Title Case ('If It Ends 1-1', via CSS capitalize) while the buzz headline chips are lowercase ('if away scores next, ...'). Unify to sentence case (capitalize first letter only) for consistency. Minor cosmetic; noticed during the v0.10.2 redesign.

title: OCR/vision parse of FIFA screenshot to cut admin toil on the import escape hatch
description: On the screenshot->admin escape hatch (predictex-4ar), vision-parse the screenshot into the existing /import preview/confirm flow to remove manual admin entry. The preview gate catches misreads. Keeps the clean source for self-serve; this is only for the genuinely non-technical fallback.

title: xox: derive bookmarklet import URL from endpoint, not hardcoded @import_url
description: ImportLive hardcodes @import_url="https://wc-predict.davewil.dev/import". Correct for prod, but a dev/staging-rendered bookmarklet would point at the wrong host. Derive from PredictexWeb.Endpoint URL config so each env renders a correct bookmarklet. Minor (single-operator deploy today). Found in xox final review.
close_reason: Bookmarklet URL now derived from PredictexWeb.Endpoint.url() so the dev page emits a localhost bookmarklet and prod emits the prod one. Was blocking local manual validation (dev page emitted a prod-pointing bookmarklet -> 404 on undeployed prod).

title: Harden Predictions.save_round_row against direct-API misuse
description: Code-review (a02) flagged two robustness gaps in admin_save_round_predictions/3 that are NOT reachable from the by-player LiveView (which always passes atom-keyed, same-round rows) but would bite a future direct caller: (1) save_round_row stamps the caller-supplied round_id without verifying the fixture belongs to that round, so a cross-round fixture would persist with a wrong denormalized round_id — admin_upsert_prediction/1 and create_prediction/2 both re-derive round_id from the fetched fixture and are immune; (2) the nil/nil and booster_on_blank clauses match atom keys only, so a string-keyed row map falls through and row.fixture_id raises KeyError. Fix: re-derive round_id per row from the fixture, and/or normalise keys at the context boundary. Low priority — current sole caller is safe.
notes:
ARCHITECTURAL SEAM (from 2026-06-18 Gather→Decide→Act review): admin_save_round_predictions/3 (predictions.ex:103-119) uses an inverted validate-then-write seam. It clears all the round's boosters, reduces over rows WRITING each via save_round_row, then scans results for {:error, :booster_on_blank} and Repo.rollback's. The booster-on-blank rule is fully determinable from the input row alone (booster=true + blank scoreline — no DB), yet is discovered post-write and undone. Safe today (the transaction prevents partial writes; correctness-neutral) but the rule can only be exercised by running the whole DB transaction — there is no pure function to test it against. This is also why the LiveView (admin_predictions_live.ex:58-84) must pattern-match on {:error, :booster_on_blank} and summarize/1 the context result instead of validating at the boundary.

FIX (subsumes gap 2 above): extract a pure validate_round_rows(rows) :: :ok | {:error, :booster_on_blank, [fixture_id]} called BEFORE Repo.transaction; only write once it returns :ok. Do key-normalisation (atom/string) inside this pure pre-check so the string-keyed KeyError (gap 2) cannot reach the writer. Gap 1 (re-derive round_id per row from the fixture) stays a writer-side fix. Net: pure decision testable without DB, validation moved to the boundary, all three robustness items closed together.
